fact: Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil, best known by her pen name George Sand, was a French novelist, memoirist and journalist.

title: Rudolf Steiner dies
subtitle: Austrian social reformer, occultist and esotericist
fact: Rudolf Joseph Lorenz Steiner was an Austrian New Age guru, philosopher, occultist, social reformer, architect, esotericist, and claimed clairvoyant.

title: Thomas Hardy dies
subtitle: English novelist and poet
fact: Thomas Hardy was an English novelist and poet.

title: Erich Ludendorff dies
subtitle: German Army officer
fact: Erich Friedrich Wilhelm Ludendorff was a Prussian-born German general and politician.

title: Kahlil Gibran dies
subtitle: Lebanese-American writer, poet, and painter
fact: Jubrān Khalīl Jubrān, usually referred to in English as Kahlil Gibran, was a Lebanese-American writer, poet and visual artist.

title: Harriet Beecher Stowe dies
subtitle: American abolitionist and author
fact: Harriet Elisabeth Beecher Stowe was an American author and abolitionist.

title: Joseph Haydn dies
subtitle: Austrian composer
fact: Franz Joseph Haydn was an Austrian composer of the Classical period.

title: Max Karl Ernst Ludwig Planck dies
subtitle: German theoretical physicist
fact: Max Karl Ernst Ludwig Planck was a German theoretical physicist.

title: Shaka Zulu dies
subtitle: Leader of the Zulu Kingdom
fact: Shaka kaSenzangakhona, also known as Shaka (the) Zulu and Sigidi kaSenzangakhona, was the king of the Zulu Kingdom from 1816 to 1828.

title: Vidkun Quisling dies
subtitle: Norwegian politician and Nazi collaborator
fact: Vidkun Abraham Lauritz Jonssøn Quisling was a Norwegian military officer, politician and Nazi collaborator who headed the government of Norway during the country's occupation by Nazi Germany during World War II.

title: Maurice Ravel dies
subtitle: French composer
fact: Joseph Maurice Ravel was a French composer, pianist and conductor.

title: Robert H. Goddard dies
subtitle: American physicist
fact: Robert Hutchings Goddard was an American physicist, inventor, and engineer credited with creating and building the world's first liquid-fueled rocket, which was successfully launched on March 16, 1926.

title: David Livingstone dies
subtitle: Scottish missionary and explorer
fact: David Livingstone was a Scottish doctor, Congregationalist, pioneer Christian missionary with the London Missionary Society, and an explorer in Africa.

title: Émile Zola dies
subtitle: French novelist, journalist, playwright, and poet
fact: Émile Édouard Charles Antoine Zola was a French novelist, journalist, playwright, the best-known practitioner of the literary school of naturalism, and an important contributor to the development of theatrical naturalism.

title: Felix Mendelssohn dies
subtitle: German composer, pianist, organist and conductor of Jewish descent
fact: Jakob Ludwig Felix Mendelssohn Bartholdy, known as Felix Mendelssohn, was a German composer, pianist, organist and conductor of the early Romantic period.

title: Stefan Zweig dies
subtitle: Austrian writer
fact: Stefan Zweig was an Austrian writer.

title: Antonín Dvořák dies
subtitle: Czech composer
fact: Antonín Leopold Dvořák was a Czech composer.

title: Paul Cézanne dies
subtitle: French painter
fact: Paul Cézanne was a French Post-Impressionist painter whose work introduced new modes of representation, influenced avant-garde artistic movements of the early 20th century and formed the bridge between late 19th-century Impressionism and early 20th-century Cubism.

title: Honoré de Balzac dies
subtitle: French novelist and playwright
fact: Honoré de Balzac was a French novelist and playwright.

title: Nathaniel Hawthorne dies
subtitle: American author
fact: Nathaniel Hawthorne was an American novelist and short story writer.

title: John Muir dies
subtitle: Scottish-American naturalist
fact: John Muir, also known as "John of the Mountains" and "Father of the National Parks", was a Scottish-born American naturalist, author, environmental philosopher, botanist, zoologist, glaciologist, and early advocate for the preservation of wilderness in the United States.

title: Katsushika Hokusai dies
subtitle: Japanese artist
fact: Katsushika Hokusai was a Japanese ukiyo-e artist of the Edo period, active as a painter and printmaker.

title: Walter Scott dies
subtitle: Scottish novelist, poet and playwright
fact: Sir Walter Scott, 1st Baronet, was a Scottish novelist, poet and historian.

title: Giuseppe Verdi dies
subtitle: Italian opera composer
fact: Giuseppe Fortunino Francesco Verdi was an Italian composer best known for his operas.

title: Piet Mondrian dies
subtitle: Dutch painter
fact: Pieter Cornelis Mondriaan, known after 1911 as Piet Mondrian, was a Dutch painter and art theoretician, who is regarded as one of the greatest artists of the 20th century.

title: Guglielmo Marconi dies
subtitle: Italian inventor and radio pioneer
fact: Guglielmo Giovanni Maria Marconi, 1st Marquess, was an Italian radio-frequency engineer, inventor, and politician known for his creation of a practical radio wave-based wireless telegraph system.

title: Joseph Bonaparte dies
fact: Joseph Bonaparte, regnal name Joseph I, was a French statesman, lawyer, diplomat and older brother of Napoleon Bonaparte.

title: Nellie Bly dies
subtitle: American journalist
fact: Elizabeth Cochrane Seaman, better known by her pen name Nellie Bly, was an American journalist who was widely known for her record-breaking trip around the world in 72 days in emulation of Jules Verne's fictional character Phileas Fogg, and for an exposé in which she worked undercover to report on a mental institution from within.

title: Erik Satie dies
subtitle: French composer and pianist
fact: Eric Alfred Leslie Satie, better known as Erik Satie, was a French composer and pianist.

title: James Watt dies
subtitle: British engineer
fact: James Watt was a Scottish inventor, engineer and chemist who improved on Thomas Newcomen's 1712 Newcomen steam engine with his Watt steam engine in 1776, which was fundamental to the changes brought by the Industrial Revolution in both his native Great Britain and the rest of the world.

title: Pierre-Auguste Renoir dies
subtitle: French painter and sculptor
fact: Pierre-Auguste Renoir was a French artist who was a leading painter in the development of the Impressionist style.

title: Victor Emmanuel II of Italy dies
fact: Victor Emmanuel II was King of Sardinia from 23 March 1849 until 17 March 1861, when he assumed the title of King of Italy and became the first king of an independent, united Italy since the 6th century, a title he held until his death in 1878.

title: Maxim Gorky dies
subtitle: Russian and Soviet writer
fact: Alexei Maximovich Peshkov, popularly known as Maxim Gorky, was a Russian and Soviet writer and proponent of socialism.

title: Robert Schumann dies
subtitle: German composer, pianist and critic
fact: Robert Schumann was a German composer, pianist, and music critic of the early Romantic era.

title: Rainer Maria Rilke dies
subtitle: Austrian poet and writer
fact: René Karl Wilhelm Johann Josef Maria Rilke, known as Rainer Maria Rilke, was an Austrian poet.

title: Édouard Manet dies
subtitle: French painter
fact: Édouard Manet was a French modernist painter.

title: Nat Turner dies
subtitle: American slave rebellion leader
fact: Nat Turner was an enslaved Black carpenter and preacher who led a four-day rebellion of both enslaved and free Black people in Southampton County, Virginia, in August 1831.

title: Sarah Bernhardt dies
subtitle: French stage actress, painter and sculptor
fact: Sarah Bernhardt was a French stage actress who starred in some of the most popular French plays of the late 19th and early 20th centuries, including La Dame aux Camélias by Alexandre Dumas fils, Ruy Blas by Victor Hugo, Fédora and La Tosca by Victorien Sardou, and L'Aiglon by Edmond Rostand.

title: Robert Koch dies
subtitle: German physician, bacteriologist
fact: Heinrich Hermann Robert Koch was a German physician and microbiologist.

title: Edgar Degas dies
subtitle: French Impressionist artist
fact: Edgar Degas was a French Impressionist artist famous for his pastel drawings and oil paintings.

title: Wilhelm I dies
subtitle: German Emperor and King of Prussia
fact: Wilhelm I was King of Prussia from 1861 and German Emperor from 1871 until his death in 1888.

title: Giacomo Puccini dies
subtitle: Italian opera composer
fact: Giacomo Antonio Domenico Michele Secondo Maria Puccini was an Italian composer known primarily for his operas.

title: Niccolò Paganini dies
subtitle: Italian violinist and composer
fact: Niccolò Paganini was an Italian violinist and composer.

title: Pedro II of Brazil dies
subtitle: 2Nd and final Emperor of Brazil
fact: Dom Pedro II, known as "the Magnanimous", was the second and final emperor of the Empire of Brazil.

title: Federico García Lorca dies
subtitle: Spanish poet, dramatist and prose writer
fact: Federico del Sagrado Corazón de Jesús García Lorca was a Spanish poet, playwright, and theatre director.

title: George Mallory dies
subtitle: English mountaineer
fact: George Herbert Leigh-Mallory was an English mountaineer who participated in the first three British Mount Everest expeditions from the early to mid-1920s.

title: Richard Strauss dies
subtitle: German composer and orchestra director
fact: Richard Georg Strauss was a German composer and conductor known for his tone poems and operas.

title: Charles XIV John of Sweden dies
fact: Charles XIV John was King of Sweden and Norway from 1818 until his death in 1844 and the first monarch of the Bernadotte dynasty.

title: Charles Baudelaire dies
subtitle: French poet and critic
fact: Charles-Pierre Baudelaire was a French poet, essayist, translator and art critic.

title: Pedro I of Brazil dies
fact: Dom Pedro I, known in Brazil and in Portugal as "the Liberator" or "the Soldier King" in Portugal, was the founder and first ruler of the Empire of Brazil from 1822 to 1831 and King of Portugal in 1826.

title: Alexander von Humboldt dies
subtitle: Prussian geographer, naturalist and explorer
fact: Friedrich Wilhelm Heinrich Alexander von Humboldt was a German polymath, geographer, naturalist, explorer, and proponent of Romantic philosophy and science.

title: Joachim Murat dies
subtitle: Grand Duke of Berg and Cleves, The king of Naples
fact: Joachim Murat was a French Army officer and statesman who served during the French Revolutionary and Napoleonic Wars.

title: Roald Amundsen dies
subtitle: Norwegian explorer; first person to reach the South Pole
fact: Roald Engelbregt Gravning Amundsen was a Norwegian explorer of polar regions.

title: Carl Benz dies
subtitle: German automotive and engine designer and manufacturer
fact: Carl Friedrich Benz was a German engine designer and automotive engineer.

title: Amedeo Modigliani dies
subtitle: Italian painter and sculptor
fact: Amedeo Clemente Modigliani was an Italian painter and sculptor of the École de Paris who worked mainly in France.

title: John Galsworthy dies
subtitle: English novelist and playwright
fact: John Galsworthy was an English novelist and playwright.

title: Auguste Rodin dies
subtitle: French sculptor
fact: François Auguste René Rodin was a French sculptor generally considered the founder of modern sculpture.

title: Jacques-Louis David dies
subtitle: French painter
fact: Jacques-Louis David was a French painter in the Neoclassical style, considered to be the preeminent painter of the era.

title: Robert Baden-Powell, 1st Baron Baden-Powell dies
fact: Lieutenant-General Robert Stephenson Smyth Baden-Powell, 1st Baron Baden-Powell,, was a British Army officer, writer, founder of The Boy Scouts Association and its first Chief Scout, and founder, with his sister Agnes, of The Girl Guides Association.

title: Charles Taze Russell dies
subtitle: Founder of the Bible Student movement
fact: Charles Taze Russell, or Pastor Russell, was an American Adventist minister from Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, and founder of the Bible Student movement.

title: Thomas Carlyle dies
subtitle: Scottish essayist, historian and philosopher
fact: Thomas Carlyle was a Scottish essayist, historian and philosopher.

title: Paul Ehrlich dies
subtitle: Jewish-German physician and scientist
fact: Paul Ehrlich was a German physician and scientist who worked in the fields of hematology, immunology and antimicrobial chemotherapy.

title: Robert Falcon Scott dies
subtitle: Royal Navy officer and explorer
fact: Captain Robert Falcon Scott was a British Royal Navy officer and explorer who led two expeditions to the Antarctic regions: the Discovery expedition of 1901–04 and the Terra Nova expedition of 1910–13.

title: José Martí dies
subtitle: Cuban poet, philosopher and nationalist
fact: José Julián Martí Pérez was a Cuban nationalist, poet, philosopher, essayist, journalist, translator, professor, and publisher, who is considered a Cuban national hero because of his role in the liberation of his country from Spain.

title: Ferdinand Foch dies
subtitle: French marchal and military theorist
fact: Ferdinand Foch was a French general, Marshal of France and a member of the Académie Française and Académie des Sciences.

title: Roger Casement dies
subtitle: Irish diplomat, activist, nationalist and poet
fact: Roger David Casement, known as Sir Roger Casement, CMG, between 1911 and 1916, was a diplomat and Irish nationalist executed by the United Kingdom for treason during World War I.

title: Báb dies
subtitle: Founder of Bábism and, according to the Baháʼís, predecessor of Baháʼu'lláh
fact: The Báb was an Iranian religious leader who founded Bábism, and is also one of the central figures of the Baháʼí Faith.

title: James Connolly dies
subtitle: Irish republican, trade unionist and socialist revolutionary
fact: James Connolly was a Scottish-born Irish republican, socialist, and trade union leader, executed for his part in the 1916 Easter Rising against British rule in Ireland.

title: Józef Piłsudski dies
subtitle: Polish politician, First Marshall, and Prime Minister
fact: Józef Klemens Piłsudski[a] was a Polish statesman who served as the Chief of State (1918–1922) and first Marshal of Poland.

title: Alfred Dreyfus dies
subtitle: French artillery officer
fact: Alfred Dreyfus was a French Army officer best known for his central role in the Dreyfus affair.

title: Paul Klee dies
subtitle: German-Swiss artist
fact: Paul Klee was a Swiss-born German artist.

title: Béla Bartók dies
subtitle: Hungarian composer and pianist
fact: Béla Viktor János Bartók was a Hungarian composer, pianist and ethnomusicologist.

title: Georges Bizet dies
subtitle: French composer
fact: Georges Bizet was a French composer of the Romantic era.

title: Rudolf Diesel dies
subtitle: German inventor and mechanical engineer
fact: Rudolf Christian Karl Diesel was a German inventor and mechanical engineer, best known for inventing the diesel engine, which burns diesel fuel; both are named after him.

title: Francis Galton dies
subtitle: British eugenist, polymath, and behavioural geneticist
fact: Sir Francis Galton was an English polymath and the originator of eugenics during the Victorian era; his ideas later became the basis of behavioural genetics.

title: Umberto I of Italy dies
subtitle: King of Italy
fact: Umberto I was King of Italy from 9 January 1878 until his assassination in 1900.

title: Alessandro Volta dies
subtitle: Italian chemist and physicist
fact: Alessandro Giuseppe Antonio Anastasio Volta was an Italian chemist and physicist who was a pioneer of electricity and power, and is credited as the inventor of the electric battery and the discoverer of methane.

title: David Hilbert dies
subtitle: German mathematician
fact: David Hilbert was a German mathematician and philosopher of mathematics and one of the most influential mathematicians of his time.

title: Francisco I. Madero dies
subtitle: Mexican revolutionary leader and president
fact: Francisco Ignacio Madero González was a Mexican businessman, revolutionary, writer, politician and statesman who served as the 37th president of Mexico from 1911 until he was deposed and assassinated in a coup d'état in February 1913.

title: Caspar David Friedrich dies
subtitle: German Romantic landscape painter
fact: Caspar David Friedrich was a German Romantic landscape painter, generally considered the most important German artist of his generation, whose often symbolic, and anti-classical work, conveys a subjective, emotional response to the natural world.

title: Peter Kropotkin dies
subtitle: Russian revolutionary socialist and philosopher
fact: Pyotr Alexeyevich Kropotkin was a Russian anarchist political philosopher and geographer known as a proponent of anarchist communism.

title: Grand Duchess Tatiana Nikolaevna of Russia dies
subtitle: Grand Duchess of Russia
fact: Grand Duchess Tatiana Nikolaevna of Russia was the second daughter of Tsar Nicholas II, the last monarch of Russia, and Tsarina Alexandra Feodorovna.

title: Bal Gangadhar Tilak dies
subtitle: Indian self-rule activist
fact: Bal Gangadhar Tilak, was an Indian nationalist and self-rule activist in the Indian independence movement.

title: Helmuth von Moltke the Elder dies
subtitle: German Field Marshal
fact: Graf Helmuth Karl Bernhard von Moltke was a Prussian Generalfeldmarschall.

title: Mikhail Bulgakov dies
subtitle: Russian author
fact: Mikhail Afanasyevich Bulgakov was a Russian and Soviet novelist and playwright.

title: Tadeusz Kościuszko dies
subtitle: Polish, Lithuanian and American military leader
fact: Andrzej Tadeusz Bonawentura Kościuszko was a Polish military engineer, statesman, and military leader who became a national hero in Poland, the United States, Lithuania, and Belarus.

title: Samuel Finley Breese Morse dies
subtitle: American inventor and painter
fact: Samuel Finley Breese Morse was an American inventor and painter.

title: Mikhail Bakunin dies
subtitle: Russian revolutionary anarchist and philosopher
fact: Mikhail Alexandrovich Bakunin was a Russian revolutionary anarchist and political philosopher.

title: Swaminarayan dies
subtitle: Founder and deity of the Swaminarayan sect
fact: Swaminarayan, also known as Sahajanand Swami, was a yogi and ascetic believed by followers to be a manifestation of Krishna or the highest manifestation of Purushottama, around whom the Swaminarayan Sampradaya developed.

title: Nestor Makhno dies
subtitle: Ukrainian anarchist revolutionary
fact: Nestor Ivanovych Makhno, also known as Bat'ko Makhno, was a Ukrainian anarchist revolutionary and the commander of the Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine during the Ukrainian War of Independence.

title: Alexander Suvorov dies
subtitle: Russian marshal
fact: Count Alexander Vasilyevich Suvorov-Rymniksky, Prince of Italy was a Russian general and military theorist in the service of the Russian Empire.

title: Gertrude Bell dies
subtitle: British traveller, writer, mountaineer, politician, archaeologist and spy
fact: Gertrude Margaret Lowthian Bell was an English writer, traveller, political officer, administrator, and archaeologist.

title: Eugène Delacroix dies
subtitle: French painter
fact: Ferdinand Victor Eugène Delacroix was a French Romantic artist who was regarded as the leader of the French Romantic school.

title: Alexander Kolchak dies
subtitle: Russian admiral and military leader of the White movement
fact: Admiral Alexander Vasilyevich Kolchak was a Russian navy officer and polar explorer who led the White movement in the Russian Civil War.

title: Ivan Turgenev dies
subtitle: Russian writer
fact: Ivan Sergeyevich Turgenev was a Russian novelist, short story writer, poet, playwright, translator and popularizer of Russian literature in the West.

title: Dante Gabriel Rossetti dies
subtitle: English poet, illustrator, painter, and translator
fact: Gabriel Charles Dante Rossetti, generally known as Dante Gabriel Rossetti, was an English poet, illustrator, painter, translator, and member of the Rossetti family.

title: Sakamoto Ryōma dies
subtitle: Japanese samurai and politician
fact: Sakamoto Ryōma was a Japanese samurai, a shishi and influential figure of the Bakumatsu, and establishment of the Empire of Japan in the late Edo period.

title: Giuseppe Mazzini dies
subtitle: Italian nationalist activist, politician, journalist and philosopher
fact: Giuseppe Mazzini was an Italian politician, lawyer, journalist, philosopher, and political activist who worked for the unification of Italy (Risorgimento) and was a spearhead of the Italian revolutionary movement.

title: Auguste Comte dies
subtitle: French philosopher, mathematician and sociologist
fact: Isidore Auguste Marie François Xavier Comte was a French philosopher, mathematician and writer who formulated the doctrine of positivism.

title: William Herschel dies
subtitle: German-born British astronomer and composer
fact: Frederick William Herschel was a German-British astronomer and composer.

title: Mary Cassatt dies
subtitle: American painter and printmaker
fact: Mary Stevenson Cassatt was an American painter and printmaker.

title: Gustave Flaubert dies
subtitle: French novelist
fact: Gustave Flaubert was a French novelist.

title: Grigory Zinoviev dies
subtitle: Ukrainian revolutionary
fact: Grigory Yevseyevich Zinoviev was a Russian revolutionary and Soviet politician.

title: Henry Morton Stanley dies
subtitle: British journalist and explorer
fact: Sir Henry Morton Stanley was a Welsh-American explorer, journalist, soldier, colonial administrator, author, and politician famous for his exploration of Central Africa and search for missionary and explorer David Livingstone.

title: Edvard Grieg dies
subtitle: Norwegian composer and pianist
fact: Edvard Hagerup Grieg was a Norwegian composer and pianist.

title: Lev Kamenev dies
subtitle: Russian revolutionary and Soviet politician
fact: Lev Borisovich Kamenev was a Russian revolutionary and Soviet politician.

title: Gioachino Rossini dies
subtitle: Italian opera composer
fact: Gioachino Antonio Rossini was an Italian composer of the late Classical and early Romantic eras.

title: Friedrich Schiller dies
subtitle: German playwright, poet, philosopher and historian
fact: Johann Christoph Friedrich von Schiller was a German playwright, poet, philosopher and historian.

title: Yakov Sverdlov dies
subtitle: Soviet politician
fact: Yakov Mikhailovich Sverdlov was a Russian revolutionary and Soviet politician.

title: Ilya Repin dies
subtitle: Russian-empire painter of Ukrainian birth
fact: Ilya Yefimovich Repin was a Ukrainian-born Russian painter.

title: Johann Strauss II dies
subtitle: Austrian composer
fact: Johann Baptist Strauss II, also known as Johann Strauss Jr., the Younger or the Son, was an Austrian composer of light music, particularly dance music and operettas, as well as a violinist.

title: Felix Dzerzhinsky dies
subtitle: Bolshevik revolutionary and founder of the first Soviet secret police organs
fact: Felix Edmundovich Dzerzhinsky, nicknamed Iron Felix, was a Soviet revolutionary and politician of Polish origin.

title: Sergei Eisenstein dies
subtitle: Soviet filmmaker
fact: Sergei Mikhailovich Eisenstein was a Soviet film director, screenwriter, film editor and film theorist.

title: Nikolai Rimsky-Korsakov dies
subtitle: Russian composer
fact: Nikolai Andreyevich Rimsky-Korsakov was a Russian composer, a member of the group of composers known as The Five.

title: Alfred Russel Wallace dies
subtitle: British naturalist, explorer, geographer, anthropologist and biologist
fact: Alfred Russel Wallace was an English naturalist, explorer, geographer, anthropologist, biologist and illustrator.

title: Chulalongkorn dies
subtitle: King of Siam
fact: Chulalongkorn, posthumously honoured as King Chulalongkorn the Great, was the fifth king of Siam from the Chakri dynasty, titled Rama V.

title: Guy de Maupassant dies
subtitle: French writer
fact: Henri René Albert Guy de Maupassant was a 19th-century French author, celebrated as a master of the short story, as well as a representative of the naturalist school, depicting human lives, destinies and social forces in disillusioned and often pessimistic terms.

title: Camille Pissarro dies
subtitle: Danish-French painter
fact: Jacob Abraham Camille Pissarro was a Danish-French Impressionist and Neo-Impressionist painter born on the island of Saint Thomas.

title: John Logie Baird dies
subtitle: Scottish inventor, demonstrating the world's first working television
fact: John Logie Baird was a Scottish inventor, electrical engineer and innovator who demonstrated the world's first mechanical television system on 26 January 1926.

title: Hector Berlioz dies
subtitle: French composer and conductor
fact: Louis-Hector Berlioz was a French Romantic composer and conductor.

title: George Curzon, 1st Marquess Curzon of Kedleston dies
subtitle: Viceroy of India and British Foreign Secretary
fact: George Nathaniel Curzon, 1st Marquess Curzon of Kedleston, known as Lord Curzon, was a British statesman, Conservative politician, explorer and writer who served as Viceroy of India from 1899 to 1905 and Foreign Secretary from 1919 to 1924.

title: Taras Shevchenko dies
subtitle: Ukrainian poet and artist
fact: Taras Hryhorovych Shevchenko was a Ukrainian poet, writer, artist, public and political figure, folklorist, and ethnographer.

title: Richard Francis Burton dies
subtitle: British explorer, army officer, writer and scholar
fact: Sir Richard Francis Burton, KCMG, FRGS, was a British explorer, army officer, writer and scholar.

title: Alberto Santos-Dumont dies
subtitle: Brazilian aviation pioneer
fact: Alberto Santos-Dumont was a Brazilian aeronaut, sportsman, inventor, and one of the few people to have contributed significantly to the early development of both lighter-than-air and heavier-than-air aircraft.

title: James Fenimore Cooper dies
subtitle: American writer
fact: James Fenimore Cooper was an American writer of the first half of the 19th century, whose historical romances depicting colonial and indigenous characters from the 17th to the 19th centuries brought him fame and fortune.

title: Pierre-Joseph Proudhon dies
subtitle: French politician, philosopher, anarchist and socialist
fact: Pierre-Joseph Proudhon was a French anarchist, socialist, philosopher, and economist who founded mutualist philosophy and is considered by many to be the "father of anarchism".

title: Modest Petrovich Mussorgsky dies
subtitle: Russian composer
fact: Modest Petrovich Mussorgsky was a Russian composer, one of the group known as "The Five."

title: Henri Bergson dies
subtitle: French philosopher
fact: Henri-Louis Bergson was a French philosopher who was influential in the traditions of analytic philosophy and continental philosophy, especially during the first half of the 20th century until the Second World War, but also after 1966 when Gilles Deleuze published Le Bergsonisme.

title: Heinrich Heine dies
subtitle: German poet, writer and literary critic
fact: Christian Johann Heinrich Heine was a Jewish born German poet, writer and literary critic.

title: John Franklin dies
subtitle: British naval officer and explorer
fact: Rear-Admiral Sir John Franklin was a British Royal Navy officer, explorer and colonial administrator.

title: Karl Liebknecht dies
subtitle: German socialist, editor, and co-founder of the Spartacus League
fact: Karl Paul August Friedrich Liebknecht was a German socialist politician and revolutionary.

title: Ernst Thälmann dies
subtitle: German communist politician, leader of Communist Party of Germany
fact: Ernst Johannes Fritz Thälmann was a German communist politician, revolutionary, and leader of the Communist Party of Germany (KPD) from 1925 to 1933.

title: Kazimir Malevich dies
fact: Kazimir Severinovich Malevich was a Russian avant-garde artist and art theorist, whose pioneering work and writing influenced the development of abstract art in the 20th century.

title: Lala Lajpat Rai dies
subtitle: Indian author and politician
fact: Lala Lajpat Rai was an Indian revolutionary, politician, and author, popularly known as Punjab Kesari.

title: Andrés Bonifacio dies
subtitle: Filipino nationalist and revolutionary hero
fact: Andrés Bonifacio was a Filipino revolutionary leader.

title: Patrick Pearse dies
fact: Patrick Henry Pearse was an Irish teacher, barrister, poet, writer, nationalist, republican political activist and revolutionary who was one of the leaders of the Easter Rising in 1916.

title: Samuel Colt dies
subtitle: American industrialist and inventor
fact: Samuel Colt was an American inventor, industrialist, and businessman who established Colt's Patent Fire-Arms Manufacturing Company and made the mass production of revolvers commercially viable.

title: Mikhail Kutuzov dies
subtitle: Field Marshal of the Russian Empire
fact: Prince Mikhail Illarionovich Golenishchev-Kutuzov-Smolensky was a field marshal of the Russian Empire.

title: Ernst Haeckel dies
subtitle: German zoologist
fact: Ernst Heinrich Philipp August Haeckel was a German zoologist, naturalist, eugenicist, philosopher, physician, professor, marine biologist and artist.

title: Stendhal dies
subtitle: French writer
fact: Marie-Henri Beyle, better known by his pen name Stendhal, was a French writer.

title: Louis Braille dies
fact: Louis Braille was a French educator and the inventor of a reading and writing system named after him, braille, intended for use by visually impaired people.

title: Constance Markievicz dies
fact: Constance Georgine Markievicz, also known as Countess Markievicz and Madame Markievicz, was an Irish revolutionary nationalist politician, suffragist and socialist who was the first woman elected to the Parliament of the United Kingdom.

title: Nicholas Roerich dies
subtitle: Russian painter, writer, archaeologist and philosopher
fact: Nikolai Konstantinovich Rerikh, better known as Nicholas Roerich, was a Russian polymath, painter, writer, archaeologist, theosophist, philosopher, and public figure.

title: Bedřich Smetana dies
subtitle: Czech composer
fact: Bedřich Smetana was a Czech composer who pioneered the development of a musical style that became closely identified with his people's aspirations to a cultural and political "revival".

title: Lu Xun dies
subtitle: Chinese novelist and essayist
fact: Lu Xun, pen name of Zhou Shuren, born Zhou Zhangshou, was a Chinese writer.

title: Humphry Davy dies
subtitle: British chemist
fact: Sir Humphry Davy, 1st Baronet was a British chemist and inventor who invented the Davy lamp and a very early form of arc lamp.

title: Vladimir Mayakovsky dies
subtitle: Russian and Soviet poet
fact: Vladimir Vladimirovich Mayakovsky was a Russian poet, playwright, artist, and actor.

title: Alfred Wegener dies
subtitle: German climatologist and geophysicist
fact: Alfred Lothar Wegener was a German climatologist, geologist, geophysicist, meteorologist, and polar researcher.

title: Abdelkader El Djezairi dies
subtitle: Algerian religious and military leader
fact: Abd al-Qadir ibn Muhyi al-Din, known as the Emir Abdelkader or Abd al-Qadir al-Hassani al-Jaza'iri, was an Algerian religious and military leader who led a struggle against the French colonial invasion of Algiers in the early 19th century.

title: Heinrich Hertz dies
subtitle: German physicist
fact: Heinrich Rudolf Hertz was a German physicist who first conclusively proved the existence of the electromagnetic waves proposed by James Clerk Maxwell's equations of electromagnetism.

title: Jacques Offenbach dies
subtitle: German-born French composer

title: Paul-Émile Lecoq de Boisbaudran dies
subtitle: French chemist
fact: Paul-Émile Lecoq de Boisbaudran, also called François Lecoq de Boisbaudran, was a self-taught French chemist known for his discoveries of the chemical elements gallium, samarium and dysprosium.
